turn the .csv into an sqlite database and load it up in datasette and now we're really cooking with fire (except some towns are sneaking through my county filters) https://t.co/0vnCtSU94B
i will take another direction, for now, I am quite happy with the amount of work that can be done in one fell swoop, and a fun CSV to play with https://t.co/UeI9vBJVbG
@boundsocket All data roads lead to python, i resist
@boundsocket like i am an idiot but something like `sed -i '0,/^.*$/s//$(cat HEADERS.csv)/' FILE.csv`

@boundsocket I was having trouble with sed on OS X giving me funny errors which lead to strange StackOverflows which lead me to brew installing gnu sed and then quickly hating sed and wanting to move on – however, this is close to what I want. But what if my new headers are in a file?
now it wont turn into geojson, weak alchemy

hey guys it was just a simple `cat eavs-decoded-headers.csv > $@;tail -n +2 eavs-trimmed.csv >> $@` that feels much healthier
why would i have to read and write a whole file through node just to change the first line but maybe I'm an idiot

if I do make this a node script it's gonna have to be some sort of interactive CLI wizard to both rewrite column names with their definitions from a codebook and also filter to only the columns you care about, but again, first I need this to work
still haven't done this successfully, regretting learning whatever `sed` is, the easiest way to do this cannot be a node script

I spent a pretty significant and shameful portion of my day figuring out a good way to replace the column headers in a file, which turned into figuring out how to overwrite just the first line in a file from BASH, which it turns out is a pretty big ask
